# Employee Access Requirements Checklist: Phase 9 - Employee Access Scope Hardening

**Purpose**: Validate that Phase 9 employee access scope requirements are complete, clear, consistent, and ready for planning.
**Created**: 2026-06-13
**Reviewed**: 2026-06-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements. It validates requirements quality only; it is not an implementation test plan.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all employee endpoint operations covered by explicit access requirements: list, detail, create, update, delete, and role assignment? [Satisfied by spec Required Access Matrix, FR-002-FR-028, and tasks T013-T046.]
- [x] CHK002 Are all four roles covered for every employee endpoint operation without relying on inherited or implied permissions? [Satisfied by spec Required Access Matrix and contract Access Matrix.]
- [x] CHK003 Are normal employee list restrictions complete enough to prevent a self-only fallback list from being interpreted as allowed behavior? [Satisfied by spec FR-002, US1 acceptance scenario 1, SC-001, and tasks T013/T016.]
- [x] CHK004 Are manager list requirements complete for direct reports, indirect reports, no-team cases, and cross-department reports? [Satisfied by spec FR-003, FR-006, Edge Cases, US2 scenarios, and tasks T020-T026.]
- [x] CHK005 Are HR/System organization-wide list and detail requirements complete for active, suspended, terminated, and soft-deleted records? [Satisfied by spec FR-004, FR-011, US3 scenarios, contract list/detail sections, and tasks T027-T033.]
- [x] CHK006 Are employee management write requirements complete for create, update, delete, role assignment, SystemAdministrator record protection, and last-active-SystemAdministrator protection? [Satisfied by spec FR-014-FR-020, FR-028, US4 scenarios, contract role endpoint, and tasks T034-T046.]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK007 Is "employee profile data" defined clearly enough to explain why broad employee list/detail access is sensitive? [Satisfied by spec Boundary Definitions and Scope.]
- [x] CHK008 Is "active direct and indirect reports" specific enough to exclude peers, unrelated employees, manager's manager, soft-deleted reports, and terminated reports? [Satisfied by spec FR-003, FR-010, US2 scenarios, and Edge Cases.]
- [x] CHK009 Is the expected `403 Forbidden` behavior for authenticated out-of-scope employee detail access stated unambiguously? [Satisfied by spec FR-013 and Assumptions.]
- [x] CHK010 Is the distinction between missing employee records and existing out-of-scope employee records clear enough to avoid 403/404 ambiguity? [Satisfied by spec FR-012, FR-013, Assumptions, and plan Detail Scope Rules.]
- [x] CHK011 Is the SystemAdministrator record protection rule clear about HR administrator limitations on update/delete while preserving create permissions for non-SystemAdministrator employees? [Satisfied by spec FR-016, FR-017, US4 scenarios, and Required Access Matrix.]
- [x] CHK012 Is last-active-SystemAdministrator protection defined clearly enough to cover update, delete, termination, status-change, and role-demotion operations before state mutation? [Satisfied by spec FR-028, SC-011, Edge Cases, and plan Protected SystemAdministrator Records and Last-Admin Guard.]

## Requirement Consistency

- [x] CHK013 Are Phase 9 access rules consistent across user stories, functional requirements, the access matrix, success criteria, and assumptions? [Satisfied after C1 remediation by spec User Scenarios, FR-001-FR-028, Required Access Matrix, SC-001-SC-011, and Assumptions.]
- [x] CHK014 Is the manager scope definition consistent with the completed Phase 8 decision of direct plus indirect reports? [Satisfied by spec Boundary Definitions, FR-001, FR-003, and Assumptions.]
- [x] CHK015 Are HR/System historical-record visibility requirements consistent between list and detail behavior? [Satisfied by spec FR-004, FR-011, US3 scenarios, and contract list/detail sections.]
- [x] CHK016 Are employee write restrictions consistent with role assignment remaining SystemAdministrator-only? [Satisfied by spec FR-014-FR-020, Required Access Matrix, and tasks T034-T046.]
- [x] CHK017 Are no-schema-change expectations consistent with the stated dependency on existing roles and manager relationships? [Satisfied by spec FR-025, Assumptions, plan Migration and Backfill Strategy, and data-model Migration Impact.]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK018 Are all success criteria measurable as objective pass/fail or percentage-based outcomes? [Satisfied by spec SC-001-SC-011.]
- [x] CHK019 Do success criteria cover the privacy goal that normal employees receive no employee-list payload? [Satisfied by spec SC-001.]
- [x] CHK020 Do success criteria cover both item leakage and count/page leakage risk after scope filtering? [Satisfied by spec FR-005, SC-003, SC-008, Assumptions, and plan Testing and Check Strategy.]
- [x] CHK021 Do success criteria cover HR/System historical list/detail visibility for terminated and soft-deleted employees? [Satisfied by spec SC-005.]
- [x] CHK022 Do success criteria cover last-active-SystemAdministrator lockout prevention as a separate measurable outcome? [Satisfied by spec SC-011, including role demotion after C1 remediation.]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK023 Are primary scenarios defined for normal employee, manager, HR administrator, and system administrator access paths? [Satisfied by spec User Stories 1-4.]
- [x] CHK024 Are alternate scenarios defined for managers with no team members and managers with cross-department reporting chains? [Satisfied by spec US2 acceptance scenario 5 and Edge Cases.]
- [x] CHK025 Are exception scenarios defined for invalid sessions, missing employee IDs, out-of-scope employees, and forbidden management writes? [Satisfied by spec Edge Cases, FR-012, FR-013, FR-015, and task T015.]
- [x] CHK026 Are management-write scenarios defined for both allowed HR/System operations and forbidden Employee/Manager operations? [Satisfied by spec User Story 4 and tasks T034-T046.]
- [x] CHK027 Are role-assignment scenarios complete for Employee, Manager, HRAdministrator, and SystemAdministrator actors? [Satisfied by spec Required Access Matrix, FR-019, FR-020, SC-006, contract role endpoint, and task T039.]

## Edge Case Coverage

- [x] CHK028 Are soft-deleted and terminated requester cases addressed in the requirements rather than assumed to be handled only by authentication? [Satisfied by spec Edge Cases and data-model Requesting Employee.]
- [x] CHK029 Are suspended requester and target employee cases clearly bounded by Phase 8 behavior and existing employee-management rules? [Satisfied by spec Edge Cases, Assumptions, and data-model Requesting Employee.]
- [x] CHK030 Are status-filter and pagination interactions defined so filtering happens within the requester's allowed scope? [Satisfied by spec FR-003-FR-005, Assumptions, contract list query, and plan List Scope Rules.]
- [x] CHK031 Are protected SystemAdministrator record update/delete cases addressed for both HRAdministrator and SystemAdministrator users? [Satisfied by spec FR-017, FR-018, US4 scenarios, and tasks T036/T037.]
- [x] CHK032 Are existing employee business-rule failures documented as remaining in force after access checks permit the action? [Satisfied by spec FR-021, US4 scenarios, plan Validation and Error Handling, and task T040.]

## Security and Privacy Requirements

- [x] CHK033 Are authorization requirements specified at business-scope level, not merely as authenticated endpoint access? [Satisfied by spec FR-001, Boundary Definitions, and plan Service Layer Separation/Technical Approach.]
- [x] CHK034 Are employee profile privacy requirements clear about avoiding exposure of out-of-scope profile data in list and detail responses? [Satisfied by spec employee profile data definition, FR-005, FR-013, and US1/US2 scenarios.]
- [x] CHK035 Are administrative lockout prevention requirements documented for the highest-privilege role? [Satisfied by spec FR-028, SC-011, plan Last-Admin Guard, contract role endpoint, and tasks T038/T039/T044/T045.]
- [x] CHK036 Are compatibility boundaries explicit enough to prevent accidental changes to routes, cookies, claims, successful response shapes, and error response shape? [Satisfied by spec FR-022, plan Constraints/API and Route Changes, and contract Compatibility Guarantees.]

## Dependencies and Assumptions

- [x] CHK037 Are dependencies on Phase 8 scope foundation explicit enough for planning to reuse existing scope services instead of redefining scope locally? [Satisfied by spec FR-001, Assumptions, and plan Dependencies on Previous Phases.]
- [x] CHK038 Are out-of-scope boundaries complete enough to prevent Phase 10 vacation, Phase 11 trip, Phase 12 lifecycle retest, or Phase 13 Swagger work from entering Phase 9? [Satisfied by spec FR-024 and Out of Scope, and plan Out of Scope.]
- [x] CHK039 Are documentation-update requirements specific enough to identify stale lifecycle/manual testing guidance about authenticated employee access? [Satisfied by spec FR-026, SC-010, plan Documentation Update, and tasks T003/T047-T049.]
- [x] CHK040 Is the no-migration assumption validated by the spec's existing role and manager relationship dependency? [Satisfied by spec FR-025, Assumptions, data-model Migration Impact, and tasks T004/T052/T056.]

## Ambiguities and Conflicts

- [x] CHK041 Are all Phase 9 clarification decisions preserved in the requirements they affect, not only in the Clarifications section? [Satisfied by spec FR-003, FR-004, FR-016-FR-020, FR-028, Required Access Matrix, SC-006, SC-011, and Assumptions.]
- [x] CHK042 Are there any remaining conflicting statements suggesting HR administrators have unrestricted authority over SystemAdministrator employee records? [Satisfied by spec FR-016-FR-018 and Required Access Matrix; no conflicting unrestricted-HR wording remains.]
- [x] CHK043 Are there any remaining statements implying normal employees may list employee records or managers may see organization-wide employee data? [Satisfied by spec FR-002, FR-003, Required Access Matrix, and User Stories 1-2; no conflicting broad-access wording remains.]
- [x] CHK044 Are there any remaining vague terms such as "broadly", "valid", or "existing filters" that need a measurable definition before planning? [Satisfied after wording cleanup: filters are status/page/pageSize, valid data is data passing existing employee business rules, and organization-wide employee access is named explicitly.]

## Notes

- All 44 checklist items were reviewed on 2026-06-13.
- No items were marked N/A.
- C1 remediation was applied before marking last-active-SystemAdministrator role-demotion items complete.
